indicators.name: several round dining tables
indicators.rationale: Dining tables are not suitable for computer work as they lack the necessary flat surface and ergonomic support for extended use.

indicators.name: marble floor
indicators.rationale: Marble flooring is slippery and not conducive to prolonged sitting, which is required for computer work.

indicators.name: stained glass windows
indicators.rationale: Stained glass windows allow natural light, which can be distracting and reduce the need for artificial lighting during computer work.

indicators.name: chairs with armrests
indicators.rationale: Chairs with armrests are not suitable for computer work as they do not provide a comfortable and ergonomic seating position.

indicators.name: high-traffic area
indicators.rationale: The presence of other people and the high-traffic nature of the area make it unsuitable for focused solo computer work.

reasoning_summary: The indoor scene is marginally adequate for computer work (Solo). The presence of dining tables, marble flooring, stained glass windows, and chairs with armrests makes it difficult to set up a suitable workstation. Additionally, the high-traffic area further undermines the suitability for focused solo work.
